q8.explanation: Cần điền/chọn thông tin đúng từ bài đọc. Ở đoạn 6, bài viết nêu: "Webster produced two major dictionaries: A Compendious Dictionary of the English Language (1806) and the much larger American Dictionary of the English Language (1828). Initially, Webster was as conservative in his spellings as Johnson had been, but he later decided that spelling reform was both possible and desirable and advocated some quite radical changes, including the omission of silent letters such as the A in bread and head, the U of built and the E of give, and the replacement of some consonants and vowels by simpler equivalents, suggesting, for example, ‘laf’ and ‘tuf’ for ‘laugh’ and ‘tough’.". Cụm conservative khớp với yêu cầu câu hỏi và đúng giới hạn từ nếu đề có quy định.
q8.evidence: Webster produced two major dictionaries: A Compendious Dictionary of the English Language (1806) and the much larger American Dictionary of the English Language (1828). Initially, Webster was as conservative in his spellings as Johnson had been, but he later decided that spelling reform was both possible and desirable and advocated some quite radical changes, including the omission of silent letters such as the A in bread and head, the U of built and the E of give, and the replacement of some consonants and vowels by simpler equivalents, suggesting, for example, ‘laf’ and ‘tuf’ for ‘laugh’ and ‘tough’._

Ở đoạn 6, bài viết nêu: "Webster produced two major dictionaries: A Compendious Dictionary of the English Language (1806) and the much larger American Dictionary of the English Language (1828). Initially, Webster was as conservative in his spellings as Johnson had been, but he later decided that spelling reform was both possible and desirable and advocated some quite radical changes, including the omission of silent letters such as the A in bread and head, the U of built and the E of give, and the replacement of some consonants and vowels by simpler equivalents, suggesting, for example, ‘laf’ and ‘tuf’ for ‘laugh’ and ‘tough’.". q9.evidence: Webster produced two major dictionaries: A Compendious Dictionary of the English Language (1806) and the much larger American Dictionary of the English Language (1828). Initially, Webster was as conservative in his spellings as Johnson had been, but he later decided that spelling reform was both possible and desirable and advocated some quite radical changes, including the omission of silent letters such as the A in bread and head, the U of built and the E of give, and the replacement of some consonants and vowels by simpler equivalents, suggesting, for example, ‘laf’ and ‘tuf’ for ‘laugh’ and ‘tough’._

q10.explanation: Cần điền/chọn thông tin đúng từ bài đọc. Ở đoạn 6, bài viết nêu: "Initially, Webster was as conservative in his spellings as Johnson had been, but he later decided that spelling reform was both possible and desirable and advocated some quite radical changes, including the omission of silent letters such as the A in bread and head, the U of built and the E of give, and the replacement of some consonants and vowels by simpler equivalents, suggesting, for example, ‘laf’ and ‘tuf’ for ‘laugh’ and ‘tough’. In Webster’s opinion, such changes would make English spelling "sufficiently correct and regular", and also, he noted as an American patriot, make American spelling quite different from British spelling. However, Webster’s new spellings found little support, and in his dictionaries he abandoned many of them in favour of more traditional spellings.". Cụm support khớp với yêu cầu câu hỏi và đúng giới hạn từ nếu đề có quy định.
q10.evidence: Initially, Webster was as conservative in his spellings as Johnson had been, but he later decided that spelling reform was both possible and desirable and advocated some quite radical changes, including the omission of silent letters such as the A in bread and head, the U of built and the E of give, and the replacement of some consonants and vowels by simpler equivalents, suggesting, for example, ‘laf’ and ‘tuf’ for ‘laugh’ and ‘tough’. In Webster’s opinion, such changes would make English spelling "sufficiently correct and regular", and also, he noted as an American patriot, make American spelling quite different from British spelling. However, Webster’s new spellings found little support, and in his dictionaries he abandoned many of them in favour of more traditional spellings._

q11.explanation: Cần điền/chọn thông tin đúng từ bài đọc. Ở đoạn 7, bài viết nêu: "In 1857, members of the Philological Society of London decided that the current dictionaries of English were unsatisfactory and incomplete, although it was not until more than 20 years later, in 1879, that an agreement was made with James Murray and the Oxford University Press for work to begin on a completely new four-volume dictionary. But when the work completed had only progressed as far as the word 'ant' after five years, it was clear that the ten years initially allocated to the project was overly optimistic.". Cụm incomplete khớp với yêu cầu câu hỏi và đúng giới hạn từ nếu đề có quy định.
q11.evidence: In 1857, members of the Philological Society of London decided that the current dictionaries of English were unsatisfactory and incomplete, although it was not until more than 20 years later, in 1879, that an agreement was made with James Murray and the Oxford University Press for work to begin on a completely new four-volume dictionary. But when the work completed had only progressed as far as the word 'ant' after five years, it was clear that the ten years initially allocated to the project was overly optimistic._

q13.explanation: Cần điền/chọn thông tin đúng từ bài đọc. Ở đoạn 1, bài viết nêu: "Three examples are Sir Thomas Elyot’s Latin–English dictionary, published in 1538 (and, interestingly, the first English book to use the word "dictionary" in its title), Hollyband’s French–English dictionary (1593) and Florio‘s 1598 Italian–English dictionary, A World of Words. However, there were also short English–English glossaries explaining difficult or technical words in books, such as the glossary of Bible words which William Tyndale attached to his 1530 translation of the first five books of the Bible.". Cụm technical khớp với yêu cầu câu hỏi và đúng giới hạn từ nếu đề có quy định.
q13.evidence: Three examples are Sir Thomas Elyot’s Latin–English dictionary, published in 1538 (and, interestingly, the first English book to use the word "dictionary" in its title), Hollyband’s French–English dictionary (1593) and Florio‘s 1598 Italian–English dictionary, A World of Words. However, there were also short English–English glossaries explaining difficult or technical words in books, such as the glossary of Bible words which William Tyndale attached to his 1530 translation of the first five books of the Bible._
